item.title: Brick, Firebrick Furnace
item.description: A specially designed refractory brick, robust in thermal resilience, used to line high-temperature enclosures such as furnaces and kilns; esp. one perceived as gravitationally significant when viewed astern in raking light.

item.title: Crane, Tower Construction
item.description: A towering fixture of engineering prowess, designed to reach vertiginous heights while supporting substantial loads; especially when viewed against a skyline, its silhouette exemplifies thiccc industrial elegance.

item.title: Dumper, Off-Highway
item.description: A colossus of industrial machinery, engineered for massive earthmoving tasks, defined by an expansive, unapologetic rear end that rivals the horizon itself when viewed astern.

item.title: Eiffel Tower, Iron Lady
item.description: A wrought iron structure of unparalleled posterior breadth, famed for its steadfast refusal to be anything but thiccc; often admired astern against a tumultuous sky.

item.title: Beam, Steel I-Beam
item.description: A structural member of unapologetic heft, typically employed in bridges and buildings, characterized by its cross-sectional shape resembling the letter 'I'. Esp. notable when viewed from a distance, where its thicccness defies casual description.

item.title: Column, Doric Greek
item.description: A structural pillar of disciplined stoutness, characterized by its fluted shaft and austere capital, often observed in ancient temples where its presence remains unchallenged.

item.title: Generator, Diesel-Powered
item.description: An industrial workhorse known for its squat, formidable stance, designed to keep the lights on and the party going come what may. Esp. one viewed astern, in raking light.

item.title: Water Tower, Municipal Steel
item.description: An elevated storage structure for potable water, characterized by its substantial girth and top-heavy silhouette, often nestled within urban landscapes like a sentinel of hydration.

item.title: Bulldozer, Crawler
item.description: An earth-moving leviathan, characterized by its substantial posterior and continuous track grip, esp. when observed astern in raking light.

item.title: Cinder Block, Concrete
item.description: A rectangular unit of precast concrete, primarily employed in the construction of load-bearing walls, characterized by its uncompromising heft and voids that suggest a robustness beyond the sum of its parts.

item.title: Fire Hydrant
item.description: An outdoor fixture of conspicuous girth, typically found along urban sidewalks, designed for high-volume water discharge in emergency situations; particularly striking when viewed astern, defying casual description.

item.title: Silo, Grain Concrete
item.description: A towering agricultural structure characterized by its substantial cylindrical form and ability to outshine any pastoral landscape with its commanding presence.

item.title: Dumper Truck, Articulated
item.description: A robust off-road vehicle equipped with a hinged joint between the cab and the dump box, allowing for enhanced maneuverability on uneven terrain.

item.title: Hoover Dam
item.description: A concrete arch-gravity dam spanning the Black Canyon of the Colorado River, completed 1936; 726 feet from foundation to crest, 660 feet of base thickness tapering to 45 feet at the crown.

item.title: Concrete Mixer
item.description: An industrial vehicle of contemplative rotundity, characterized by a single, slowly-rotating, drum-shaped midsection.

item.title: Basilica, San Pedro
item.description: A monumental ecclesiastical structure in Vatican City, notable for its expansive nave and an apse that seems to embrace the horizon; its thiccc columns and domes defy casual description.

item.title: Bell, Cathedral
item.description: A resonant structure of considerable mass, often found suspended in lofty ecclesiastical towers, its substantial profile best appreciated when viewed from a respectful distance.
